name: Caspio Low-Code Database Application API
what_it_does: Cloud database and low-code application platform with REST API — enabling citizen developers to build web database applications, forms, reports, portals, and workflows without traditional coding, with hosting included, powerful data management, user authentication, and API access for integration with external systems.
use_cases: Reading and writing database records via Caspio REST API for external integration
Triggering Caspio automation and workflow steps from external system events
Querying application data for custom business intelligence and reporting
Managing application tables and records programmatically for bulk data operations
Integrating Caspio web database apps with enterprise systems via REST API
not_for: Complex mobile app development (use Glide or FlutterFlow for mobile)
Enterprise BPM and complex process automation (use Appian or Nintex)
Marketing websites without database (use Webflow or Squarespace)
best_when: A business analyst or operations team needs to build and host web database applications with forms, reports, and user portals without IT department involvement, especially for data management and reporting scenarios.
avoid_when: You need mobile apps, complex process automation, modern UI beyond data entry/reports, or developer-controlled custom application architecture.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials flow. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. No webhooks — must poll for data changes. REST API provides full CRUD for tables. No fine-grained scopes — full account access with client credentials.

pricing.notes: San Jose-based company. Founded 2000. Bootstrapped and profitable. Pioneer in no-code database applications. 15,000+ customers including healthcare, education, and government. Includes hosting (Caspio hosts the apps). DataPages (app components) is the core UX model. Strong in mid-size organizations that need database-driven web portals. Long track record makes it reliable choice for data management apps.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per plan; 300 requests/minute typical

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for tables, records, files, and users. OAuth2 bearer token. Good documentation. No webhooks — polling required for real-time integration. Full CRUD on all database tables. OAuth2 client credentials — simpler than authorization code flow. DataPage components not API-configurable.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Record reads fully idempotent. Table reads idempotent. Record creation not idempotent — use primary key lookup before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: No webhooks — must implement polling for data-driven integration
OAuth2 client credentials — no user-level auth for API, account-level only
DataPage components (forms, reports) are UI-only — not API-configurable
Hosting included but proprietary — cannot deploy outside Caspio infrastructure
Founded 2000 — older UI patterns may feel dated for modern web app needs

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, HIPAA, GDPR compliant. US data residency. OAuth2 client credentials. HIPAA Business Associate Agreement (BAA) available for healthcare. No fine-grained scopes — implement separate accounts per integration need. Database records can include sensitive PII — implement data classification.
